title: महाकाली स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री महाकाली स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ क्रीं काल्यै नमः॥

ध्यानम् -
करालवदनां घोरां मुक्तकेशीं चतुर्भुजाम्।
कालिकां दक्षिणां दिव्यां मुण्डमालाविभूषिताम्॥
सद्यश्छिन्नशिरः खड्गवामाधोर्ध्वकराम्बुजाम्।
अभयं वरदं चैव दक्षिणोर्ध्वाधःपाणिकाम्॥

ब्रह्मोवाच -

महाकालीं नमस्यामि खड्गमुण्डधरां शिवाम्।
सर्वसिद्धिप्रदां देवीं कालिकां प्रणमाम्यहम्॥१॥

महाकाले महाघोरे महाशक्तिमयि प्रभे।
महामोहहरे देवि महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२॥

महाविद्ये महामाये महामन्त्रप्रबोधिनि।
महाप्रलयसंहारकारिणि प्रणतोऽस्म्यहम्॥३॥

दशमुखदशभुजां दशपादां भीमरूपिणीम्।
खड्गं चक्रं गदां शूलं चापं बाणं च धारिणीम्॥४॥

शिरः खेटकमुद्गरं महाघण्टां च बिभ्रतीम्।
महाकालीं यजेत् सा हि दशमहाविद्यासु प्रथमा॥५॥

त्वं विद्या परमा शक्तिः कालस्य कालनाशिनी।
कालरात्रिर्महाकाली योगनिद्रा सनातनी॥६॥

त्वत्तः सम्भवते विश्वं त्वय्येव प्रतितिष्ठति।
त्वय्येव लीयते सर्वं त्वं शक्तिः सर्वकारणम्॥७॥

मधुकैटभनाशार्थं ब्रह्मणा समुपस्तुता।
विष्णोः प्रबोधनार्थं या निद्रारूपेण संस्थिता॥८॥

महामारी महाचण्डी महादेवी महासुरी।
महाक्रोधा महारौद्री महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥९॥

श्मशानवासिनी घोरा मुण्डमालाविभूषिता।
शवासना शवारूढ़ा महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१०॥

रक्तदन्तां विशालाक्षीं पीनोन्नतपयोधराम्।
लोलजिह्वां महारावां महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥११॥

रक्तबीजवधे देवि चामुण्डे मुण्डमालिनि।
शुम्भनिशुम्भसंहन्त्रि महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१२॥

कंकालमालिनी घोरा मुक्तकेशी दिगम्बरी।
महाभयविनाशा च महाकालि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१३॥

भक्तानामभयप्राप्तिर्भक्तानां सर्वसम्पदः।
भक्तानां दुःखनाशश्च त्वत्प्रसादान्महेश्वरि॥१४॥

यस्तु भक्त्या महाकालीं स्तौति नित्यं समाहितः।
सर्वशत्रुक्षयं कृत्वा लभते परमां गतिम्॥१५॥

॥ इति श्री ब्रह्मवैवर्तपुराणे महाकाली स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mahakali Stotram ||

Om Krim Kalyai Namah.

Dhyanam -
Karala-vadanam Ghoram Mukta-keshim Chatur-bhujam.
Kalikam Dakshinam Divyam Munda-mala-vibhushitam.
Sadyash Chhinna-shirah Khadga-vama-dhordhva-karambujam.
Abhayam Varadam Chaiva Dakshina-urdhva-adhah-panikam.

Brahma Uvacha -

Mahakalim Namasyami Khadga-munda-dharam Shivam.
Sarva-siddhi-pradam Devim Kalikam Pranamamy Aham.||1||

Mahakale Mahghore Mahashakti-mayi Prabhe.
Mahamoha-hare Devi Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||2||

Mahavidye Mahamaye Mahamantra-prabodhini.
Mahapralaya-samhara-karini Pranato'smyaham.||3||

Dasha-mukha-dasha-bhujam Dasha-padam Bhima-rupinim.
Khadgam Chakram Gadam Shulam Chapam Banam Cha Dharinim.||4||

Shirah Khetakam Udgaram Maha-ghantam Cha Bibhratim.
Mahakalim Yajet Sa Hi Dasha-mahavidyasu Prathama.||5||

Tvam Vidya Parama Shaktih Kalasya Kala-nashini.
Kalaratrir Mahakali Yoga-nidra Sanatani.||6||

Tvattah Sambhavate Vishvam Tvayy Eva Pratitishthati.
Tvayy Eva Liyate Sarvam Tvam Shaktih Sarva-karanam.||7||

Madhu-kaitabha-nashaartham Brahmana Samupastuta.
Vishnoh Prabodhana-artham Ya Nidra-rupena Samsthita.||8||

Mahamari Mahachandi Mahadevi Mahasuri.
Mahakrodha Maharaudri Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||9||

Shmashana-vasini Ghora Munda-mala-vibhushita.
Shava-asana Shava-arudha Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||10||

Rakta-dantam Vishalakshim Pinonnatapayo-dharam.
Lola-jihvam Maharavam Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||11||

Raktabija-vadhe Devi Chamunde Munda-malini.
Shumbha-nishumbha-samhantri Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||12||

Kankala-malini Ghora Mukta-keshi Digambari.
Maha-bhaya-vinasha Cha Mahakali Namo'stu Te.||13||

Bhaktanam Abhaya-praptir Bhaktanam Sarva-sampadah.
Bhaktanam Duhkha-naashash Cha Tvat-prasadan Maheshvari.||14||

Yas Tu Bhaktya Mahakalim Stauti Nityam Samahitah.
Sarva-shatru-kshayam Kritva Labhate Paramam Gatim.||15||

|| Iti Shri Brahmavaivarta-purane Mahakali Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ब्रह्मा बोले - खड्ग और मुण्ड धारण करने वाली शिवा, सर्वसिद्धिप्रदा महाकाली को मैं नमस्कार करता हूँ।

हे महाकाले! महाघोरे! महाशक्तिमयी! महामोहहारिणी! महाविद्ये! महामाये! महामन्त्रों को प्रबुद्ध करने वाली! महाप्रलय-संहारकारिणी! प्रणाम।

दशमुखी, दशभुजा, दशपादा, भीमरूपिणी, खड्ग-चक्र-गदा-शूल-चाप-बाण-शिर-खेटक-मुद्गर-घण्टा धारिणी महाकाली दशमहाविद्या में प्रथम हैं।

आप परम विद्या-शक्ति हैं, काल की भी कालनाशिनी, कालरात्रि, योगनिद्रा, सनातनी हैं। आपसे विश्व उत्पन्न होता है, आपमें स्थित रहता है, आपमें ही लीन होता है।

ब्रह्मा ने मधु-कैटभ वध के लिए और विष्णु को जगाने के लिए निद्रारूपा आपकी स्तुति की थी।

श्मशानवासिनी, मुण्डमालाभूषिता, शवारूढ़ा, रक्तदन्ता, लोलजिह्वा, कंकालमालिनी, दिगम्बरी महाकाली को नमस्कार।

भक्तों को अभय, सम्पद और दुःखनाश आपकी कृपा से होता है। जो भक्ति से नित्य स्तुति करे, वह शत्रुक्षय कर परमगति पाए।
benefits: Invokes Mahakali, the first of the Dasha Mahavidyas in her ten-armed form
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies, negative forces, and black magic influences
Traditionally recited for removal of mahamoha (great delusion) and grants spiritual awakening
Invokes the blessing of fearlessness, wealth, and removal of all sorrows to devotees
Invokes ultimate liberation (parama gati) through devoted recitation
Traditionally invoked for protection from untimely death, evil spirits, and all supernatural threats
description: Mahakali Stotram — महाकाली स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Kali. Attributed to Brahmavaivarta Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to invoke Mahakali, the first of the Dasha Mahavidyas in her ten-armed form, bestow fearlessness, wealth, and removal of all sorrows to devotees, and destroy all enemies, negative forces, and black magic influences. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Kali Puja, Diwali, and Navratri. At 15 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Mahakali Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Mahakali Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful prayer to Goddess Mahakali, first Mahavidya, for fearlessness and destruction of all evil.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mahakali Stotram and what is its scriptural source?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mahakali Stotram is a fifteen-verse Sanskrit hymn preserved in the Brahmavaivarta Purana, recited by Brahma at the time of the Madhu-Kaitabha episode when he prayed to Yoganidra Mahakali to release Vishnu from the cosmic sleep so that the two demons could be slain. The stotra opens with Om Krim Kalyai Namah, includes a four-line dhyana describing her ten-faced, ten-armed cosmic form, and proceeds with the famous declaration Mahakalim namasyami khadga-munda-dharam shivam. It is one of the most authoritative Mahakali hymns and identifies her explicitly as the first of the ten Mahavidyas (Dasha-mahavidyasu prathama). (Sources: Brahmavaivarta Purana Prakriti Khanda; traditional Shakta commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Mahakali Stotram different from the shorter Kali Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: While both texts praise the Goddess in her dark fierce form, they differ in source, length, iconography and emphasis. This Mahakali Stotram is from the Brahmavaivarta Purana and is fifteen verses long. It uniquely describes Mahakali in her cosmic ten-faced, ten-armed, ten-footed (dasha-mukha dasha-bhuja dasha-pada) form holding ten weapons including khadga, chakra, gada, shula, chapa, bana, shirah, khetaka, mudgara and mahaghanta — the universal cosmic destroyer form invoked at pralaya. The Kali Stotram is fourteen verses from general Tantra Shastra emphasising the more familiar four-armed cremation-ground Kali. The Mahakali version is more philosophical and pralaya-cosmic. (Sources: Brahmavaivarta Purana; Tantra Shastra comparison.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Madhu-Kaitabha story connected with this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Madhu-Kaitabha episode appears in the first chapter of the Devi Mahatmya (Durga Saptashati) and is also retold in several Puranas. At the start of creation, while Vishnu lay on Shesha in Yoganidra (cosmic sleep) on the cosmic ocean, two asuras Madhu and Kaitabha emerged from the wax of his ears and threatened to destroy Brahma seated on the lotus from Vishnu's navel. Brahma, unable to wake Vishnu, prayed to the Yoganidra herself — the cosmic sleep-shakti who is identical with Mahakali — with this very stotra, asking her to withdraw from Vishnu so he could awaken and slay the demons. Mahakali responded, Vishnu awoke, and the demons were slain. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya Chapter 1; Brahmavaivarta Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Mahakali called the first of the Dasha Mahavidyas?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 5 of this stotra explicitly states Mahakalim yajet sa hi dasha-mahavidyasu prathama — Mahakali should be worshipped, for she is the first among the ten Mahavidyas. The standard Dasha Mahavidya enumeration is Kali, Tara, Tripura Sundari, Bhuvaneshvari, Bhairavi, Chhinnamasta, Dhumavati, Bagalamukhi, Matangi and Kamala. Mahakali stands first because she is the cosmic dissolver of time itself (kalasya kala-nashini), the substratum from which all other forms of the Devi emerge as differentiated manifestations, and the primordial Adya Shakti who pre-exists all creation. The Mahavidyas as a group are said to have manifested from Mahakali during the Sati-Daksha episode. (Sources: Mahabhagavata Purana; Brihaddharma Purana; Mahakala Samhita.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the stotram teach about Mahakali's metaphysical nature?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 6 and 7 contain the deepest metaphysical statements: Tvam vidya parama shaktih kalasya kala-nashini — you are the supreme knowledge-power, the destroyer of even Time itself; Kalaratrir Mahakali Yoganidra Sanatani — you are the night of dissolution, Mahakali, the cosmic sleep, the eternal one; Tvattah sambhavate vishvam tvayy eva pratitishthati, tvayy eva liyate sarvam tvam shaktih sarva-karanam — from you the universe arises, in you it abides, in you alone it dissolves, you are the shakti and the cause of all. This is pure Advaita Shakta metaphysics: Mahakali as the non-dual absolute beyond which nothing exists. (Sources: Brahmavaivarta Purana verses 6-7.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Mahakali Stotram phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 14 and 15 state that by Mahakali's grace devotees obtain abhaya (fearlessness), sarva-sampadah (all forms of prosperity), duhkha-nasha (destruction of all sorrow), and ultimately parama gati (the supreme spiritual goal — liberation). Verse 15 specifies Yas tu bhaktya Mahakalim stauti nityam samahitah, sarva-shatru-kshayam kritva labhate paramam gatim — the devotee who praises Mahakali daily with concentrated devotion destroys all enemies (inner and outer) and attains the supreme state. Traditional commentaries add removal of black magic, protection from untimely death, victory in tantric sadhana, and ultimate non-dual realisation. (Sources: Brahmavaivarta Purana phalashruti; traditional Shakta commentaries.)
